subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, IDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, WFP/UNICEF) + Guatemalan official (Banguat, INE) + Guatemalan/Spanish-language press (read indirectly).
Macro stability and a reform-minded president besieged by an entrenched, corrupt establishment; a 2026 institutional reset partly eased the near-term siege.

Standout drags: a deeply captured rule of law, extreme child malnutrition/inequality, and seismic/storm exposure; prudent fiscals, a credible central bank, and a young population offset. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on political detail.

economic.notes: Conservative fiscals and a credible central bank, with chronic under-investment the flip side.

fiscal_state.notes: Very low debt (~27-30% GDP) and a small deficit on a conservative tradition; the flip side is chronic under-spending on development. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible central bank with disciplined policy. (IMF; Banguat)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The quetzal is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised, with IMF-flagged supervision/AML gaps. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Coffee/sugar/bananas plus textiles and nearshoring, with very large remittances (~19% of GDP); stable but low-value-added and remittance-dependent. (Banguat)

institutional.notes: A deeply captured rule of law and a criminalised press, with usable statistics.

rule_of_law.notes: A deeply captured 'pacto de corruptos' with weaponised prosecution; a 2026 attorney-general exit eased the near term but the courts and legislature remain captured. (TI 2025; WJP; InsightCrime)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and IMF-cited, weaker than OECD peers. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and clientelist, with a low tax take starving capacity. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Free in form but criminalised, with a prominent editor jailed and journalists exiled. (RSF; APG)

political_social.notes: A reformist president under establishment siege, against extreme malnutrition and inequality.

political_stability.notes: A reform president (elected 2023) survived coup-by-lawfare; a 2026 institutional reset partly eases the siege, but the coalition is weak and the establishment entrenched. (Atlantic Council; Americas Quarterly)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'partly free' - formal freedoms with real pressure on activists and press. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited routes; a 'safe third country' taking more US deportation flights. (MPI; State Dept)

social_cohesion.notes: Among the world's highest child chronic malnutrition (especially among indigenous Maya), with high inequality and deep marginalisation. (WFP; UNICEF)

demographics.notes: Very young - a dividend if jobs materialise, else a migration/jobs strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic US partner with an open economy, against gang/narco-transit violence.

alliance_reliability.notes: A pragmatic US partner on migration/security, Taiwan-recognising. (State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: No war; gang/narco-transit violence (scored under crime). (ACLED)

physical_practical.notes: Drought/storm and volcanic/seismic exposure, weak healthcare with a malnutrition crisis, and elevated crime.

climate.notes: Dry-corridor drought plus dual-coast storms plus highland landslides, high vulnerability, low readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Weak and uneven, with a chronic malnutrition/stunting crisis and thin rural coverage. (World Bank; WFP)

infrastructure.notes: Poor-to-moderate, with uneven roads and ports. (IDB; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: A homicide rate ~16/100k and rising extortion, below the regional peak but elevated. (InsightCrime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against high seismic/storm exposure, with World-Bank contingent finance. (World Bank)

summary: A study in contrasts: a genuinely stable macro economy - very low debt, a credible central bank, low inflation, investment-grade-adjacent prudence, large remittances, and a young population - alongside a deeply captured rule of law. A reform-minded president elected in 2023 survived a coup-by-lawfare and a 2026 institutional reset eased the near-term siege, but the courts and legislature remain in the grip of a corrupt establishment and the press is criminalised. Among the world's highest child malnutrition (especially among indigenous Maya), deep inequality, drought, volcanic/seismic hazard, and elevated crime weigh against the macro strength.
